author: pythonic
content:
Coming from JS? The single biggest mental shift in Python:

There is no `let`/`const`, no curly braces, no semicolons. Indentation IS the syntax. A block is whatever is indented under a colon.

That's not a style preference — bad indentation is a SyntaxError. The language is forcing the formatting your linter was nagging you about in JS.

comments.author: duck_typer
comments.content: the colon-then-indent thing clicks fast. what got me was that whitespace mixing (tabs vs spaces) is a real error. pick 4 spaces and never look back.

comments.author: list_comp
comments.content: and `f"{name}"` is just a template literal with a different prefix. backtick → f-quote. same idea.

author: list_comp
content:
Stop writing `for` loops to build lists. The list comprehension is `.map()` and `.filter()` in one line, and it's the Pythonic default.

`result = [transform(x) for x in items if keep(x)]`

Read it left to right: what you keep, where it comes from, the condition. Once it clicks you'll see loops as the verbose option.
code:
squares = [n*n for n in range(10) if n % 2 == 0]
# [0, 4, 16, 36, 64]

comments.author: pythonic
comments.content: just don't nest three of them. at that point a real loop is more readable and that matters more than looking clever.

author: duck_typer
content:
HOT TAKE: `try/except` is not error handling of last resort in Python — it's a normal control-flow tool.

The culture is EAFP: Easier to Ask Forgiveness than Permission. You try the thing and catch the failure, instead of checking a dozen conditions first (LBYL). It reads cleaner and avoids race conditions.

comments.author: list_comp
comments.content: caveat: catch the SPECIFIC exception (except KeyError:), never a bare `except:`. bare except swallows your Ctrl-C and your bugs.

author: pythonic
content: SPOT THE BUG: this function accumulates results across calls when it shouldn't. Why?
code:
def add_item(item, bucket=[]):
    bucket.append(item)
    return bucket

add_item(1)  # [1]
add_item(2)  # [1, 2]  <- ?!

comments.author: duck_typer
comments.content: mutable default argument. the `[]` is created ONCE when the function is defined, not per call. classic Python footgun.

comments.author: list_comp
comments.content: fix: default to None, then `bucket = bucket or []` inside. burn this into your memory.
